title: Guajava aquatica
taxonomy: Guajava aquatica belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the family Rubiaceae, Guajava aquatica is classified under the genus Psidium, which includes various species of guavas. This species is further classified as Psidium guajava, a synonym for Guajava aquatica. The classification of Guajava aquatica is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and fruit size. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Guajava aquatica.
uses: Guajava aquatica is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves are often applied topically to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In traditional medicine, Guajava aquatica is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Its antimicrobial properties make it effective against bacterial and fungal infections. Additionally, the plant is used to treat digestive issues like dysentery and stomach ulcers.
benefits: Guajava aquatica is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion due to its high antioxidant and fiber content. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its leaves and fruits are rich in vitamins and minerals, making it a nutritious addition to a balanced diet. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, Guajava aquatica has been studied for its potential to protect against certain types of cancer.

constituents: Guajava aquatica contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. parts: Guajava aquatica is known to have various medicinal properties, and the parts commonly used for medicinal purposes include the leaves and fruit. preparations: Guajava aquatica is traditionally used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. side_effects: Guajava aquatica, also known as the water guava, may cause allergic reactions in some individuals, such as skin irritation, itching, and hives. intro: Guajava aquatica, also known as water guava or guavasteen, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the Myrtaceae family and is native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Guajava aquatica include guavin, flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Guajava aquatica is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional folk remedies in the form of decoctions and poultices.
